title: Calotis breviradiata
taxonomy: Calotis breviradiata is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Calotis, which is a group of shrubland and woodland daisies native to Australia. The plant is further classified as a member of the tribe Calotidinae. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This classification indicates the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics of Calotis breviradiata within the plant kingdom.

intro: Calotis breviradiata, commonly known as the short-rayed calotis or Australian calotis, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Calotis breviradiata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as salves and creams to harness its therapeutic benefits.
